vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially inaccurate. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are absent or have suffered total systemic loss, replaced by a single, unidentified, solitary leaf specimen in a black pot.

plant_audit.P1: Shows minimal change; stable but low vitality.
plant_audit.P3: Rabbit anchor remains stable; primary leaf shows signs of chlorosis and potential edge necrosis.
plant_audit.Unidentified_Specimen: A single, lanceolate leaf remains in the black pot. It appears to be the sole survivor of a failed propagation or previous planting.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears consistently dark, suggesting high moisture content or lack of light-induced evaporation.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of white particulate matter (likely perlite or eggshell fragments) across all pot surfaces.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No moss or secondary sprouts detected; the environment appears sterile or struggling to support secondary growth.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Noticeable decline in the turgidity of the solitary leaf in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Further darkening of the leaf tissue; potential onset of senescence.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: The environment has entered a low-light 'rested state'. The solitary leaf shows signs of postural collapse (drooping).

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of decline. The lack of light and potential over-saturation of the soil is leading to the loss of specimens. The solitary leaf in the black pot is the most critical indicator of failing health.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of eggshell fragments suggests an attempt at soil amendment or pest deterrence that has not yielded visible botanical improvement.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently in a state of severe stress. The Pothos (P3) is the only stable element, though it shows signs of nutrient or light deficiency. The other pots have suffered significant losses, with only a single, struggling leaf remaining in the black pot. The environment is dark and likely too damp, inhibiting recovery.
